summary: Codex recovers from a MID-STREAM bridge tool failure: first call fails, model retries the same command, stream continues, turn completes with the build id
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.clean_stream:
I’ll read the build ID now.
BUILD_ID_a9383897392e

agent_review.notes: Codex: tool fails once, model retries, recovers correctly. Clean, terse, build id correct. tool_names includes native "exec" calls alongside "execute_shell_command" — expected, not a defect: Codex's native functions.exec is structurally unremovable, and this test doesn't run in read-only sandbox mode, so native reads are allowed here by design (see strictBridgeOnly=false for Codex).
